evals.name: first-run-bootstrap
evals.prompt: A setup script has been run that created a sandbox fixture at $FIXTURE_DIR. $FIXTURE_DIR/fork is a git clone of a fork with no `.upstream-version` file. $FIXTURE_DIR/upstream is a local git repo acting as the upstream (use its path as the upstream URL). Run the upstream-sync workflow from inside $FIXTURE_DIR/fork with the upstream URL as the argument. The environment variable UPSTREAM_SYNC_DRY_RUN=1 is set, so do everything except the actual push and MR creation — print the MR body to stdout instead. Save everything you print to $OUTPUT_DIR/stdout.txt. When finished, also copy $FIXTURE_DIR/fork/.upstream-version (if it exists) and the output of `git -C $FIXTURE_DIR/fork log --oneline --all` to $OUTPUT_DIR/.
evals.expected_output: On a first run with no `.upstream-version`, the skill should (a) create `.upstream-version` containing `UPSTREAM_REPO=<fixture upstream path>` and `UPSTREAM_VERSION=v1.1.0`, (b) commit the tracking file on a sync branch, (c) NOT merge upstream's v1.1.0 history retroactively, (d) print an MR body describing this as a bootstrap.

evals.name: clean-upgrade-no-conflict
evals.prompt: A setup script has been run that created a sandbox fixture at $FIXTURE_DIR. $FIXTURE_DIR/fork is a git clone of a fork tracking upstream tag v1.0.0 (recorded in `.upstream-version` using the new key/value format). Upstream has released v1.1.0 with non-conflicting changes. Run the upstream-sync workflow from inside $FIXTURE_DIR/fork with $FIXTURE_DIR/upstream as the upstream URL argument. UPSTREAM_SYNC_DRY_RUN=1 is set — print the MR body to stdout instead of pushing or creating an MR. Save everything you print to $OUTPUT_DIR/stdout.txt. Also copy $FIXTURE_DIR/fork/.upstream-version, $FIXTURE_DIR/fork/docs/tutorial.md (if merged), $FIXTURE_DIR/fork/src/core.py, $FIXTURE_DIR/fork/src/fork_feature.py, and the output of `git -C $FIXTURE_DIR/fork log --oneline --all` to $OUTPUT_DIR/.
evals.expected_output: Skill creates a sync branch, merges v1.1.0 cleanly (no conflicts), updates `.upstream-version` to `UPSTREAM_REPO=<fixture upstream path>` and `UPSTREAM_VERSION=v1.1.0`, preserves fork's FORK.md and fork_feature.py, gains upstream's new docs/tutorial.md and the farewell() function. MR body says 'Clean merge, no conflicts.' and dry-run output includes release-note comment content or a clear note that release notes could not be fetched for the local fixture.

evals.name: conflict-upgrade-intelligent-resolution
evals.prompt: A setup script has been run that created a sandbox fixture at $FIXTURE_DIR. $FIXTURE_DIR/fork is a git clone of a fork tracking upstream tag v1.0.0 and contains a `DOWNSTREAM_CHANGES.md` entry documenting that `config.json` must keep `fork_feature: true`. Upstream has released v1.1.0 which edits `config.json` — specifically changing the `url` field and adding a `retries` field. The fork has independently added a `fork_feature: true` field to the same region of `config.json`. Merging will conflict. Run the upstream-sync workflow from inside $FIXTURE_DIR/fork with $FIXTURE_DIR/upstream as the upstream URL argument. UPSTREAM_SYNC_DRY_RUN=1 is set — print the MR body to stdout instead of pushing. Save everything you print to $OUTPUT_DIR/stdout.txt. Also copy $FIXTURE_DIR/fork/config.json (the final resolved version), $FIXTURE_DIR/fork/.upstream-version, $FIXTURE_DIR/fork/DOWNSTREAM_CHANGES.md, and the output of `git -C $FIXTURE_DIR/fork log --oneline --all` to $OUTPUT_DIR/.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads DOWNSTREAM_CHANGES.md, merges v1.1.0, resolves the conflict in config.json by preserving upstream's url change + retries addition AND the documented fork_feature field. Resulting JSON is valid and contains all four fields (timeout, url=http://new.example.com, retries=3, fork_feature=true). `.upstream-version` uses key/value format with UPSTREAM_VERSION=v1.1.0. MR body lists config.json as a resolved conflict and explains that a documented downstream change was preserved.

evals.name: downstream-feature-superseded-by-upstream
evals.prompt: A setup script has been run that created a sandbox fixture at $FIXTURE_DIR. $FIXTURE_DIR/fork tracks upstream tag v1.0.0 and has `DOWNSTREAM_CHANGES.md` documenting a fork-only telemetry opt-out feature. Upstream v1.1.0 implements an equivalent telemetry opt-out with a different code path, causing a conflict in `settings.yml`. Run upstream-sync from inside $FIXTURE_DIR/fork with the upstream URL argument and UPSTREAM_SYNC_DRY_RUN=1. Save stdout to $OUTPUT_DIR/stdout.txt and copy the final `settings.yml`, `.upstream-version`, and `DOWNSTREAM_CHANGES.md` to $OUTPUT_DIR/.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes from DOWNSTREAM_CHANGES.md that the fork-only feature is now implemented upstream, chooses the upstream implementation as canonical, avoids keeping duplicate fork logic, and includes a prominent MR warning under 'Downstream changes superseded by upstream'. `.upstream-version` is written in key/value format.

evals.name: github-release-notes-comment
evals.prompt: A setup script has been run that created a sandbox fixture whose upstream remote URL is `https://github.com/example/project.git`, LAST_TAG is v1.0.0, and LATEST_TAG is v1.2.0. Assume `gh release view` can return release bodies for v1.1.0 and v1.2.0 and the GitLab MR is created successfully. Run upstream-sync in dry-run mode and save stdout to $OUTPUT_DIR/stdout.txt, including the MR body and any release-note comment body the skill would post.
evals.expected_output: Skill fetches or composes release notes for every related tag in v1.0.0..v1.2.0, prepares a separate MR comment headed `Upstream release notes: v1.0.0 → v1.2.0`, includes both v1.1.0 and v1.2.0 sections, and falls back gracefully if any tag lacks GitHub release notes.
